task_completion.description: Verify that the parent Maven project 'toolkit-parent' is created in Eclipse.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Eclipse Project Explorer or Package Explorer, a project named 'toolkit-parent' is visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'toolkit-core' module is created with the required utility classes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Eclipse Project Explorer, a module named 'toolkit-core' is visible, containing 'StringUtils.java' and 'MathUtils.java' within the 'com.startup.toolkit.core' package.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'toolkit-app' module is created with the 'Main' class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Eclipse Project Explorer, a module named 'toolkit-app' is visible, containing 'Main.java' within the 'com.startup.toolkit.app' package.

task_completion.description: Verify that the multi-module project builds successfully using 'mvn package'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or Eclipse Console window shows the execution of 'mvn package' (or similar Maven build command) with a 'BUILD SUCCESS' message for the parent project and both modules.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Main class executes correctly and prints the expected results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Console or a terminal window shows the output of running the Main class, specifically displaying 'Hello' (capitalized 'hello') and '3628800' (factorial of 10).

integrity.description: Check that the agent used Eclipse IDE to create the project structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Eclipse IDE interface being used to manage the projects, edit code, or view the project structure, rather than just using a text editor.

integrity.description: Check that the build and execution outputs are genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The console or terminal outputs for the Maven build and Java execution should look like standard tool outputs, not manually typed 'echo' commands.

integrity.description: Check that the Main class actually calls the utility methods instead of hardcoding the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the Main.java editor is visible, it should show calls to 'StringUtils.capitalize' and 'MathUtils.factorial', not 'System.out.println("Hello")' or 'System.out.println(3628800)'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project root: /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/toolkit-parent. Parent Group ID: com.startup.toolkit, Version: 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.
